text.title: django-selectors Failure Modes
text.summary: Five concrete antipatterns observed in selector code reviews. Each maps to a rule from `01-core-rules.xml`.

antipattern.description: Selector returns `list[Model]` (`return list(qs)`) instead of QuerySet.
antipattern.symptom: Callers cannot add `.filter(...)` or `.order_by(...)` without re-issuing a brand-new query; pagination bypasses the optimised query and loads every row into memory.
antipattern.root-cause: Author wants "explicit materialisation" or fears stale querysets. Materialisation defeats the entire point — selectors are designed to compose at the call site.
antipattern.fix: Return `QuerySet[Model]` annotated. Callers slice / filter / paginate as needed. If the caller really needs a list, it calls `list(selector(...))`.

antipattern.symptom: AttributeError downstream when callers forget the None check; or silent 200 OK responses with empty body for missing resources.
antipattern.root-cause: "Avoid exceptions in business logic" applied incorrectly. The DoesNotExist contract is Django-wide; raising matches stdlib semantics.
antipattern.fix: Let DoesNotExist propagate. Views convert it to 404 via `get_object_or_404` or a DRF exception handler. Service functions that handle "missing" cases call `qs.first()` explicitly to signal intent.

antipattern.description: Selector takes positional arguments: `def order_list(user, status=None)`.
antipattern.symptom: Call sites grow ambiguous as the function gains parameters; refactoring breaks callers silently by swapping argument order.
antipattern.root-cause: Pythonic default carries over from view helpers / utility functions; styleguide rule overlooked.
antipattern.fix: Add `*,` after `self`-less signature: `def order_list(*, user, status=None)`. Every call site becomes `order_list(user=u, status=s)` — self-documenting.

antipattern.symptom: Slow endpoints on large tables; database CPU spikes; logs show COUNT(*) on millions of rows just to know if at least one exists.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treats QuerySet like a list; doesn't know about `.exists()`.
antipattern.fix: Replace with `qs.exists()`. Generates `SELECT 1 FROM ... LIMIT 1` — constant time.

antipattern.description: Selector function performs `.save()`, `.update()`, `.delete()`, or calls `.delay()` on a Celery task.
antipattern.symptom: Test setup that calls a selector for assertion mutates DB; selectors fail under read-only replicas; idempotency assumptions violated.
antipattern.root-cause: Author conflates "function with DB access" with "selector". Write functions belong in `services.py`.
antipattern.fix: Move the write into `services.py`. Selector returns the data; the service decides what to mutate.
